calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-16-2016 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication And Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Jun-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES J. O'DONNELL OF O'DONNELL & SMITH, TEL. NO. 1-925-935-1707 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JAMES F. HANNAWALT, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-554-3913 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication - Motion for Summary Judgment is Granted. The City satisfied its initial summary judgment burden by presenting admissible evidence that there was no causal connection between the alleged property defects and the City's asserted negligence and Juice's unanticipated assault on plaintiff Carter Waite. (UMF 10, 11, 12, 13.) "A public entity may be liable if it maintained its property in such a way as to increase the risk of criminal activity. However, liability is imposed only when there is some defect in the property itself and a causal connection is established between the defect and the injury. Harmful third party conduct is not characterized as a dangerous condition absent some concurrent contributing defect in the property itself." (Zelig v. County of Los Angeles (2002) 27 Cal.4th 1112.) The evidence presented by the City shows that the location of the exits and the employee monitoring station did not cause the injuries suffered by Mr. Waite. Juice's punch to Mr. Waite was sudden and unanticipated. The evidence presented by the City also shows that there is no causal connection between the asserted failure to warn and the injuries suffered by Mr. Waite. Mr. Waite has not presented any evidence creating a triable issue on causation on either of his claims. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk
